Bug / Task Description: RingBuffer.RemoveLastWhere in bco-core/ringbuffer.go removes the most recent entry matching a predicate and compacts the newer entries into the freed slot. After removing an entry that is not the newest, the entries that were newer end up in wrong positions, so a later Entries() readout is corrupted (duplicated/garbage values, a stale slot left live). Removing the newest or oldest still appears to work, masking the defect.
Commit Subject: fix(ringbuffer): tighten compaction shift in RemoveLastWhere
